label: Modern India
pitch:
Gandhi returns to India in 1915 and transforms a colonial subject population into a nonviolent mass movement. The Salt March of 1930 captivated the world, but India gains independence in 1947 came with the traumatic Partition of India that killed up to two million people. From those ashes, the Indian Constitution adopted in 1950 built the world's largest democracy — and Economic liberalization begins in 1991 unleashed a transformation that is still accelerating.

🔑 Key Facts:
• Gandhi assassinated in 1948 by a Hindu nationalist plunged the newly independent nation into mourning just months after freedom
• Indira Gandhi becomes PM in 1966 and dominates Indian politics for two decades — until Indira Gandhi assassinated by her own bodyguards in 1984
• The IT boom and Bangalore rises turned India into the world's back office, and Chandrayaan-3 lands on Moon made India the first nation to reach the lunar south pole
• The Green Revolution begins in 1965 transformed India from famine-prone to food-surplus in a single generation

🤔 Why It Matters: India proved that democracy can survive in conditions of staggering diversity — over a billion people, hundreds of languages, deep religious divisions — and its tech workforce now powers companies across the globe.

🎲 Wild Card: Rabindranath Tagore wins Nobel in 1913 as the first non-European literature laureate — he wrote the national anthems of both India AND Bangladesh, making him the only person in history to author two different countries' anthems.

spans.label: British Raj
spans.description: Direct Crown rule replaced the East India Company after the 1857 rebellion, governing the subcontinent through a viceroy and civil service. British infrastructure modernized India while extractive policies drained its wealth.

spans.label: Independence Movement
spans.description: Beginning with the founding of the Indian National Congress, the freedom struggle evolved from elite petitions to Gandhi's mass nonviolent campaigns. The movement ultimately ended nearly a century of British rule in 1947.

spans.label: Nehruvian Era
spans.description: Jawaharlal Nehru shaped independent India through democratic socialism, secularism, and non-alignment in foreign policy. His government built heavy industry, dams, and scientific institutions while navigating Partition's aftermath.

spans.label: Indira Gandhi Era
spans.description: Indira Gandhi dominated Indian politics with populist programs like bank nationalization and the Green Revolution, but also declared a controversial Emergency suspending civil liberties. Her assassination in 1984 triggered widespread communal violence.

spans.label: Economic Liberalization
spans.description: Finance Minister Manmohan Singh dismantled the License Raj amid a balance-of-payments crisis, opening India to foreign investment and global markets. The reforms fueled rapid GDP growth and India's rise as an IT and services powerhouse.

spans.label: Coalition Era
spans.description: A quarter-century of multiparty coalition governments, from the 1989 hung parliament through the NDA and UPA, during which no single party held an outright Lok Sabha majority.

spans.label: Modi Era
spans.description: The return of single-party dominance under Narendra Modi's BJP, marked by demonetization, GST, the abrogation of Article 370, and a civilizational-nationalist reframing of Indian identity.

events.label: Sepoy Mutiny
events.description: Indian soldiers of the Bengal Army rebelled over greased rifle cartridges, igniting the largest armed challenge to British rule in 19th-century India and ending East India Company governance.

events.label: Bahadur Shah Zafar Exiled
events.description: The last Mughal emperor, made figurehead of the 1857 rebellion, was tried for treason and exiled to Rangoon, formally ending the 330-year Mughal line.

events.label: British Raj Established
events.description: The Government of India Act 1858 transferred Company territories to the Crown, created the Secretary of State for India, and made Queen Victoria sovereign of British India.

events.label: Indian Penal Code Enacted
events.description: Thomas Macaulay's codification of Indian criminal law on English lines became the governing criminal code, with amendments, until it was replaced in 2023.

events.label: First Railway Line Opens
events.description: The Bombay-to-Thane line opened the Indian railway network, which grew from zero to 25,000 miles by 1900, stitching the subcontinent into a single economic and political space.

events.label: Suez Canal Shortens the India Route
events.description: The Suez Canal cut the sea distance between Britain and India by roughly 4,500 miles, deepening colonial economic integration and accelerating the export economy.

events.label: Victoria Proclaimed Empress of India
events.description: The Royal Titles Act made Queen Victoria Empress of India, formalizing the symbolic apex of the Raj and the durbar political theatre that came with it.

events.label: Great Famine of 1876-78
events.description: A monsoon failure and laissez-faire colonial policy produced a famine that killed 5-10 million people across the Deccan and Madras while India remained a net food exporter.

events.label: Brahmo Samaj and the Sati Ban
events.description: Ram Mohan Roy's Brahmo Samaj combined Vedanta monotheism with social reform; his campaign produced the 1829 Company ban on sati, the immolation of widows.

events.label: Arya Samaj Founded
events.description: Dayananda Saraswati's Arya Samaj launched a Vedic-revivalist Hindu reform movement that shaped later cultural nationalism in northern India.

events.label: Indian National Congress Founded
events.description: Seventy-two delegates convened by retired civil servant A. O. Hume founded the Congress as a moderate debating body; it became the vehicle of the freedom struggle.

events.label: Taj Mahal Palace Hotel Opens
events.description: Jamsetji Tata's Bombay hotel became a symbol of Indian commercial ambition under the Raj and, a century later, a target of the 2008 Mumbai attacks.

events.label: Rabindranath Tagore Wins the Nobel
events.description: Tagore's self-translated Gitanjali won the Nobel Prize in Literature, the first to any non-European, a watershed for Indian cultural self-conception.

events.label: Partition of Bengal
events.description: Curzon split Bengal along religious-demographic lines, triggering the Swadeshi mass movement; the partition was reversed in 1911.

events.label: Swadeshi Movement Begins
events.description: The boycott of British goods and promotion of Indian-made substitutes was the first sustained mass mobilization of the nationalist period and the template for Gandhi's later campaigns.

events.label: All-India Muslim League Founded
events.description: Founded at Dhaka to lobby for separate Muslim political representation, the League would become the vehicle of the Pakistan movement.

events.label: The Surat Congress Split
events.description: Moderates and extremists came to blows on the conference floor over the Swadeshi resolution, splitting the Congress for nearly a decade.

events.label: Morley-Minto Reforms
events.description: The Indian Councils Act expanded legislative councils and introduced separate Muslim electorates, embedding communal representation in colonial constitutional structure.

events.label: Imperial Capital Moves to Delhi
events.description: The capital was shifted from Calcutta to a new city south of Delhi, partly to escape Bengali nationalist agitation; New Delhi was built 1911-1931.

events.label: India in the First World War
events.description: India sent 1.5 million soldiers and laborers to the war on the understanding that constitutional advance would follow British victory.

events.label: Lucknow Pact
events.description: Congress and the Muslim League agreed a joint demand for self-government with reserved Muslim seats, the high-water mark of Hindu-Muslim political cooperation.

events.label: Rowlatt Act
events.description: The extension of wartime detention-without-trial powers into peacetime triggered Gandhi's first nationwide hartal and the protests that led to Amritsar.

events.label: Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
events.description: General Dyer ordered 1,650 rounds fired into a trapped crowd at Amritsar; Congress counted at least 1,000 dead, ending British constitutional credibility with Indian nationalism.

events.label: Government of India Act 1919
events.description: The Montagu-Chelmsford reforms introduced provincial dyarchy and a partly elected legislature, judged inadequate by the radicalizing Congress.

events.label: New Delhi Inaugurated
events.description: Lutyens and Baker's imperial capital was formally inaugurated, the architectural statement of a Raj that would last only sixteen more years.

events.label: Gandhi Returns to India
events.description: Gandhi returned from twenty-one years in South Africa with the satyagraha method, an Indian-peasant self-presentation, and the khadi program.

events.label: Champaran Satyagraha
events.description: Gandhi's first Indian campaign, in support of Bihar's indigo tenant farmers, proved satyagraha could organize illiterate peasant populations.

events.label: Khilafat Movement
events.description: The Indian Muslim campaign to preserve the Ottoman caliphate allied with Non-Cooperation to produce the only sustained unified Hindu-Muslim mass movement of the late Raj.

events.label: Non-Cooperation Movement
events.description: Gandhi's first national satyagraha boycotted British schools, courts, legislatures, honors, and goods, with Congress membership reaching two million.

events.label: Chauri Chaura Incident
events.description: A Non-Cooperation crowd burned a police station and killed 22 policemen; Gandhi unilaterally called off the movement, a decision bitterly contested within Congress.

events.label: Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh Founded
events.description: The RSS was founded at Nagpur as a Hindu-nationalist cultural organization; it would become the organizational core of the later Hindu-nationalist movement.

events.label: Purna Swaraj Declaration
events.description: The Lahore Congress declared complete independence the goal and set 26 January as Independence Day, later chosen as Republic Day.

events.label: The Salt March
events.description: Gandhi's theatrically engineered 240-mile walk to Dandi broke the British salt monopoly by mass civil defiance and brought world attention to the freedom struggle.

events.label: Dharasana Satyagraha
events.description: Police systematically beat over 2,000 unresisting satyagrahis at the Dharasana salt works; Webb Miller's eyewitness dispatch shifted American opinion against the Raj.

events.label: Round Table Conferences
events.description: Three London conferences on India's constitutional future produced the Government of India Act 1935 but not the independence the Congress now demanded.

events.label: The Poona Pact
events.description: Gandhi's fast forced Ambedkar to trade separate Dalit electorates for reserved seats within general constituencies, a substantive and unresolved disagreement.

events.label: Government of India Act 1935
events.description: The largest single piece of British colonial legislation created provincial autonomy with elected Indian ministries and framed Indian politics until 1947.

events.label: Congress Provincial Ministries
events.description: Congress won majorities in seven of eleven provinces and held office for 28 months, the future leaders' first sustained experience of executive government.

events.label: Lahore Resolution
events.description: The Muslim League called for 'Independent States' in the Muslim-majority regions of British India, the constitutional opening to Partition.

events.label: Cripps Mission
events.description: Britain's wartime offer of post-war dominion status with a provincial opt-out was rejected by Congress; Gandhi called it 'a post-dated cheque on a failing bank.'

events.label: Quit India Movement
events.description: The Congress demanded immediate British withdrawal; the entire senior leadership was arrested within 24 hours and the resulting uprising was the most violent satyagraha.

events.label: Bose Leads the Indian National Army
events.description: Subhas Chandra Bose reorganized the INA from Japanese-captured Indian POWs and led an Axis-supported invasion attempt at Imphal-Kohima in 1944.

events.label: Bengal Famine of 1943
events.description: War policy, a cyclone, and the loss of Burmese rice imports produced a famine that killed 2-3 million people while India was a net food exporter.

events.label: The Red Fort INA Trials
events.description: Court-martials of INA officers became a cause célèbre; the Royal Indian Navy mutinied in February 1946 and the British realized the army could no longer be relied on.

events.label: Cabinet Mission Plan
events.description: Britain's proposed three-tier federation was accepted then repudiated by both Congress and the League, collapsing the last unitary settlement.

events.label: Direct Action Day
events.description: Jinnah's call to demonstrate Muslim resolve became the Great Calcutta Killings, a four-day communal riot that killed about 4,000 people.

events.label: The Mountbatten Plan
events.description: The new Viceroy compressed the transfer-of-power schedule to ten weeks and set partition with the Punjab and Bengal divided along demographic lines.

events.label: The Radcliffe Line
events.description: Cyril Radcliffe, who had never been to India, drew the Punjab and Bengal borders in five weeks; the award was published two days after independence.

events.label: India Gains Independence
events.description: At midnight on 15 August 1947 the British Raj ended; Nehru's 'Tryst with Destiny' speech consecrated the moment as the killing peaked in the Punjab.

events.label: Partition of India
events.description: The division of British India killed 500,000-2 million people and displaced 15 million, the largest forced migration in human history outside war or famine.

events.label: Integration of the Princely States
events.description: Patel and V. P. Menon secured the accession of 562 of 565 princely states through pressure, privy purses, and the threat of isolation.

events.label: First Kashmir War
events.description: Pashtun tribal irregulars invaded Kashmir; the Hindu Maharaja acceded to India in exchange for military intervention, leaving the territory divided along a UN ceasefire line.

events.label: Junagadh Accession
events.description: The Muslim Nawab of Hindu-majority Junagadh acceded to Pakistan; a 1948 plebiscite confirmed accession to India.

events.label: Operation Polo
events.description: The Indian Army occupied independent-minded Hyderabad in five days; associated communal violence killed tens of thousands.

events.label: Gandhi Assassinated
events.description: Nathuram Godse, a former RSS and Hindu Mahasabha member, shot Gandhi at a Birla House prayer meeting in revenge for the Partition-assets fast.

events.label: RSS Banned After the Assassination
events.description: The RSS was briefly banned after Gandhi's killing; the ban was lifted in 1949 on a commitment to remain a cultural organization.

events.label: Nehru Becomes First Prime Minister
events.description: Nehru held the office continuously until his death in 1964, building secular democracy, planned development, and non-alignment.

events.label: Ambedkar Drafts the Constitution
events.description: Nehru placed the Dalit jurist and Gandhi-critic B. R. Ambedkar at the head of the drafting committee, making the claim of full Dalit citizenship politically real.

events.label: Indian Constitution Adopted
events.description: The longest written constitution of any sovereign nation abolished untouchability, granted universal adult suffrage, and established a secular federal republic.

events.label: First General Election
events.description: The first universal-franchise election of 173 million voters returned the Congress and demonstrated that mass democracy could function in conditions of mass illiteracy.

events.label: First Five-Year Plan Launched
events.description: India committed to a Soviet-influenced model of state-led industrialization with public-sector heavy industry as the commanding heights.

events.label: First IIT Established
events.description: IIT Kharagpur founded the engineering-education infrastructure that would later staff Indian industry and Silicon Valley.

events.label: States Reorganisation Act
events.description: Internal state boundaries were redrawn along linguistic lines, defusing 1950s linguistic separatism and producing the federal structure that has held since.

events.label: Ambedkar's Buddhist Conversion
events.description: Ambedkar converted to Buddhism with around half a million Dalit followers two months before his death, a structural rejection of the Hindu caste order.

events.label: First IIM Established
events.description: The Indian Institutes of Management were founded to build a managerial-education base for the planned economy.

events.label: Annexation of Goa
events.description: The Indian Army ended 451 years of Portuguese rule in Goa in a 36-hour operation, completing the territorial decolonization of the subcontinent.

events.label: Non-Aligned Movement Founded
events.description: India, Yugoslavia, and Egypt constituted the Non-Aligned Movement at Belgrade, the institutional form of Nehruvian non-alignment.

events.label: Sino-Indian War
events.description: A PLA offensive overran Indian forward positions in days, destroyed the non-alignment doctrine's credibility, and broke Nehru politically.

events.label: Bhakra Nangal Dam Completed
events.description: Nehru's 'temple of modern India' was one of the world's highest gravity dams, the symbol of the planned-development era's infrastructure ambition.

events.label: Nehru Dies
events.description: Never fully recovered from the 1962 defeat, Nehru died after seventeen unbroken years as Prime Minister, ending the founding era.

events.label: Shastri Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: The soft-spoken UP politician chosen for his inoffensiveness launched the Green Revolution and led India through the 1965 war.

events.label: Green Revolution Begins
events.description: Imported Mexican high-yielding wheat, fertilizer, and irrigation ended India's recurring famines and achieved cereal self-sufficiency by 1971.

events.label: Second Indo-Pakistani War
events.description: A 17-day conventional war triggered by Pakistani infiltration of Kashmir ended in a stalemate settled at the Tashkent Conference.

events.label: Tashkent Declaration
events.description: The Soviet-brokered settlement returned captured territory; Shastri died of a heart attack hours after signing it.

events.label: Indira Gandhi Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: The Congress Syndicate chose Nehru's daughter expecting a pliant figure they could control, one of the great miscalculations of post-independence politics.

events.label: Bank Nationalization
events.description: Indira nationalized the fourteen largest private banks in an overnight ordinance, a massively popular move that split the Congress.

events.label: The Congress Split of 1969
events.description: The Syndicate expelled Indira; her Congress (R) retained the larger share of MPs and won the political battle, remaking the party in her image.

events.label: ISRO Founded
events.description: The Indian Space Research Organisation was established, beginning a low-cost space program that would later reach the Moon and Mars.

events.label: Garibi Hatao Landslide
events.description: Indira's snap election on a single-issue 'Remove Poverty' campaign took 352 of 518 Lok Sabha seats, giving her a commanding majority.

events.label: Bangladesh Liberation War
events.description: Indian forces entered East Pakistan and accepted the surrender of 93,000 Pakistani troops in 13 days, creating independent Bangladesh.

events.label: Abolition of Privy Purses
events.description: The 26th Amendment abolished the constitutionally guaranteed pensions of the former princes, completing the break with the 1947 settlement.

events.label: Smiling Buddha Nuclear Test
events.description: India's first nuclear detonation made it the first weapons-capable state outside the original five and triggered Pakistani crash development.

events.label: Kesavananda Bharati Judgment
events.description: The Supreme Court ruled Parliament could not amend the Constitution's 'basic structure,' the doctrine that would later constrain executive overreach.

events.label: JP's Total Revolution
events.description: Jayaprakash Narayan's mass agitation, including a call for police and army disobedience, was the immediate trigger for the Emergency.

events.label: Allahabad Verdict Against Indira
events.description: The Allahabad High Court voided Indira's 1971 election for electoral malpractice, the first time a sitting Indian PM was found guilty by a court.

events.label: The Emergency Declared
events.description: Indira proclaimed a 21-month national emergency, jailing the opposition, censoring the press, and amending the Constitution to entrench executive power.

events.label: Sikkim Joins India
events.description: A referendum and constitutional amendment merged the Himalayan kingdom of Sikkim as India's 22nd state.

events.label: 42nd Constitutional Amendment
events.description: The 'mini-constitution' amendment restricted judicial review, added 'socialist' and 'secular' to the preamble, and entrenched emergency-era executive power.

events.label: Forced Sterilization Campaign
events.description: Sanjay Gandhi's coercive family-planning campaign performed an estimated eight million sterilizations with at least 1,800 documented deaths.

events.label: Aryabhata, India's First Satellite
events.description: India launched its first satellite on a Soviet rocket, an early milestone of the ISRO program during the Emergency years.

events.label: Janata Party Formed
events.description: Four opposition groups merged on an explicit anti-Emergency program to contest the 1977 election as a single vehicle.

events.label: The 1977 Election
events.description: The Janata Party won 295 seats and ended thirty years of unbroken Congress rule; Indira lost her own seat, and Indian democracy survived its first authoritarian interlude.

events.label: 44th Constitutional Amendment
events.description: The Janata government narrowed emergency powers, made the right to life non-suspendable, and reversed most of the 42nd Amendment's executive entrenchment.

events.label: Indira Returns to Power
events.description: After the Janata coalition collapsed, Indira won 353 of 529 seats on the implicit promise that the Congress could at least manage the bureaucracy.

events.label: Sanjay Gandhi Dies
events.description: Indira's chosen heir died in a small-plane crash, drawing the reluctant airline pilot Rajiv Gandhi into politics.

events.label: Bharatiya Janata Party Founded
events.description: The BJP was founded from the Jana Sangh wing of the collapsed Janata Party, the party that would dominate 21st-century Indian politics.

events.label: Operation Blue Star
events.description: The Indian Army's assault on the Golden Temple to remove the militant preacher Bhindranwale killed hundreds and left an unhealed wound in the Sikh community.

events.label: Indira Gandhi Assassinated
events.description: Two Sikh bodyguards shot Indira in revenge for Operation Blue Star, triggering the organized anti-Sikh pogrom in Delhi.

events.label: Anti-Sikh Pogrom
events.description: Organized violence with Congress political coordination and police complicity killed at least 3,000 Sikhs in Delhi over three days.

events.label: Bhopal Gas Disaster
events.description: The methyl isocyanate release at the Union Carbide plant was the worst single industrial accident in human history, killing 3,000 immediately and many thousands more over decades.

events.label: Rajiv Gandhi Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: Rajiv was sworn in within hours of his mother's death and won 414 of 542 seats on sympathy voting, the largest single-party majority in any Indian election.

events.label: Shah Bano Case
events.description: The Rajiv government legislatively reversed a Supreme Court maintenance ruling, supplying the empirical anchor for the BJP's 'appeasement' critique of Congress secularism.

events.label: Babri Masjid Unlocked
events.description: A district court ordered the disputed Ayodhya mosque opened for Hindu worship, igniting six years of escalating mobilization on both sides.

events.label: Bofors Scandal
events.description: Alleged kickbacks in a Swedish howitzer contract politically destroyed the Rajiv government before any conviction was secured.

events.label: IPKF in Sri Lanka
events.description: India's peacekeeping deployment against the LTTE escalated to 80,000 troops and killed 1,200 Indian soldiers in a strategic failure.

events.label: Mandal Commission Implemented
events.description: V. P. Singh's announcement of 27% OBC reservations made the OBC vote a coherent bloc and produced the regional caste parties that dominated Hindi-belt politics.

events.label: Advani's Rath Yatra
events.description: Advani's chariot procession calling for a Ram temple at Ayodhya generated communal violence and brought down the V. P. Singh government.

events.label: Rajiv Gandhi Assassinated
events.description: An LTTE suicide bomber killed Rajiv at a campaign rally near Madras, in revenge for the IPKF intervention in Sri Lanka.

events.label: Narasimha Rao Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: The unexpected choice, considered an ineffectual elder, would oversee the most consequential economic reforms in Indian history.

events.label: The 1991 Balance-of-Payments Crisis
events.description: Foreign reserves fell to three weeks of import cover; 47 tons of gold were airlifted to London and pledged to avert default.

events.label: Economic Liberalization Begins
events.description: Manmohan Singh's July 1991 budget devalued the rupee, abolished the License Raj, and opened India to foreign investment, ending the planned-economy era.

events.label: Babri Masjid Demolished
events.description: About 150,000 BJP-VHP karsevaks demolished the 464-year-old mosque, triggering the worst Hindu-Muslim violence since Partition.

events.label: Bombay Riots and Bombings
events.description: Communal riots killed over 900 people, mostly Muslim; the March 1993 retaliatory bombings killed 257 in the first major terror attack on Indian cities.

events.label: First Non-Nehru-Gandhi Full Term
events.description: The Rao government became the first non-Nehru-Gandhi government to complete a full five-year term, then lost the 1996 election.

events.label: Satellite Television Arrives
events.description: The Gulf War and the arrival of satellite channels broke the state broadcasting monopoly and reshaped Indian public culture in the liberalization decade.

events.label: The Coalition Decade Begins
events.description: Four prime ministers held office between 1996 and 1999 as no party could form a stable government and the BJP slowly built its alliance.

events.label: Vajpayee and the NDA
events.description: Vajpayee's National Democratic Alliance gave the BJP its first sustained central government, setting aside its core ideological issues for coalition support.

events.label: Pokhran-II Nuclear Tests
events.description: Five underground detonations made India formally nuclear-armed and triggered reciprocal Pakistani tests two weeks later.

events.label: Kargil War
events.description: Pakistani troops disguised as insurgents occupied Indian Himalayan posts; the Indian counter-offensive was the first nuclear-era Indo-Pakistani conflict.

events.label: Golden Quadrilateral Launched
events.description: The 5,846-km highway network connecting the four major metros was the largest physical-infrastructure investment since the colonial railways.

events.label: IT Boom and Bangalore Rises
events.description: Y2K-driven software contracts grew the Indian IT-services industry from $5 billion in 2000 to over $150 billion by 2020, making Bangalore the export-services hub.

events.label: 2001 Parliament Attack
events.description: A Jaish-e-Mohammed and Lashkar-e-Taiba attack on Parliament triggered a year-long Indo-Pakistani military standoff.

events.label: Three New States Created
events.description: Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, and Uttarakhand were carved out, the largest internal reorganization since 1956.

events.label: 2002 Gujarat Violence
events.description: Anti-Muslim communal violence under Chief Minister Narendra Modi killed at least 1,000 people and shaped his political identity for fifteen years.

events.label: Delhi Metro Opens
events.description: The first line of the Delhi Metro opened, the flagship of a wave of urban mass-transit construction across Indian cities.

events.label: India Shining and the 2004 Upset
events.description: The BJP's economic-reform campaign failed to reach the rural voter; the Congress-led UPA won and Manmohan Singh became Prime Minister.

events.label: Manmohan Singh Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: The architect of the 1991 reforms became Prime Minister with Sonia Gandhi as Congress leader, the most coherent governing partnership of the post-1991 era.

events.label: MGNREGA Enacted
events.description: The rural-employment guarantee became the largest workfare program in human history by enrollment, providing a rural consumption floor in drought years.

events.label: Right to Information Act
events.description: The sweeping freedom-of-information law became a foundational instrument of civil-society accountability, with six million requests filed annually by the mid-2010s.

events.label: Indo-US Civil Nuclear Agreement
events.description: The deal acknowledged India's nuclear-weapons status outside the NPT and marked the US choosing India over Pakistan as its primary South Asian partner.

events.label: Mumbai 26/11 Attacks
events.description: Ten Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists infiltrating by sea from Karachi killed 166 people across multiple targets in a 60-hour siege.

events.label: Chandrayaan-1 Lunar Mission
events.description: India's first lunar probe entered orbit and detected water on the Moon, the first space agency outside the major blocs to do so.

events.label: UPA Re-elected
events.description: A stronger 2009 mandate, navigating the 2008 financial crisis with relative success, was read as endorsement of the UPA welfare-and-growth model.

events.label: 2G Spectrum Scandal
events.description: A CAG report alleged Rs 1.76 lakh crore in below-market telecom-license allocations; the Supreme Court cancelled 122 licenses, defining UPA-2.

events.label: Commonwealth Games Scandal
events.description: Contract irregularities and cost overruns at the Delhi Commonwealth Games undermined the Congress's managerial-competence claim.

events.label: Anna Hazare Movement
events.description: Hazare's anti-corruption hunger strikes produced mass middle-class mobilization and spawned both the Aam Aadmi Party and the BJP's anti-corruption framing.

events.label: Nirbhaya Protests
events.description: The fatal Delhi gang rape produced nationwide protests and the 2013 criminal-law reforms on sexual violence.

events.label: The 2013 Rupee Crisis
events.description: Growth fell to 4.7%, the rupee hit historical lows, and inflation stayed above 10%, ending the perception that the high-growth era would continue.

events.label: Narendra Modi Becomes Prime Minister
events.description: The BJP won the first single-party Lok Sabha majority since 1984, reducing the Congress to its worst-ever 44 seats.

events.label: Jan Dhan and the JAM Trinity
events.description: Bank accounts for 500 million unbanked Indians, combined with Aadhaar and mobile numbers, built the digital-welfare delivery infrastructure.

events.label: Swachh Bharat Mission Launched
events.description: The national sanitation campaign built about 110 million toilets in five years, with real implementation gaps but substantial political symbolism.

events.label: Mangalyaan Reaches Mars
events.description: India's Mars Orbiter Mission was the first Asian mission to Mars and the first by any agency to succeed on its first attempt, at a fraction of comparable costs.

events.label: Uri Attack and Surgical Strikes
events.description: After the Uri attack killed 19 soldiers, India's first acknowledged cross-border strike signaled an end to the Manmohan-era policy of strategic restraint.

events.label: Demonetization of Banknotes
events.description: Modi invalidated 86% of cash currency by value with five hours notice; 99.3% was redeposited, but digital payments adoption accelerated sharply.

events.label: Goods and Services Tax
events.description: The GST replaced a tangle of central and state indirect taxes with a single harmonized system, removing internal trade barriers between Indian states.

events.label: Aadhaar and the Privacy Judgment
events.description: The Supreme Court affirmed a fundamental right to privacy and narrowed the permissible uses of the Aadhaar biometric-identity system.

events.label: Doklam Standoff
events.description: A 73-day Indo-Chinese military standoff at a tri-junction with Bhutan ended by mutual disengagement, prefiguring the 2020 Galwan clash.

events.label: Statue of Unity Unveiled
events.description: The 182-metre statue of Vallabhbhai Patel, the world's tallest, became the signature monument of the Modi-era civilizational-nationalist program.

events.label: Pulwama-Balakot Crisis
events.description: After a suicide bombing killed 40 paramilitaries at Pulwama, Indian air strikes inside Pakistan were the first since 1971.

events.label: Modi Re-elected
events.description: A stronger 303-seat BJP majority gave Modi the authority to pursue the core Hindu-nationalist program the NDA coalition had partially blunted.

events.label: Article 370 Abrogated
events.description: The abrogation of Kashmir's special status and its division into two Union Territories was accompanied by the longest internet shutdown by any democracy.

events.label: Citizenship Amendment Act
events.description: The act offered expedited citizenship to non-Muslim refugees from three neighboring states, triggering the months-long Shaheen Bagh protests.

events.label: Ayodhya Title Verdict
events.description: The Supreme Court awarded the disputed Ayodhya site to the Ram temple trust and allotted an alternative parcel for a mosque, resolving the 70-year dispute.

events.label: Shaheen Bagh Protests
events.description: The months-long sit-in led mainly by Muslim women became the defining symbol of opposition to the CAA-NRC framework before the COVID lockdown dispersed it.

events.label: COVID-19 Pandemic and Lockdown
events.description: One of the world's most stringent lockdowns stranded tens of millions of migrant workers; the 2021 Delta wave produced an estimated 3-5 million excess deaths.

events.label: Galwan Valley Clash
events.description: Hand-to-hand combat in Ladakh killed 20 Indian and at least 40 Chinese soldiers, the first lethal Indo-Chinese border engagement since 1975.

events.label: Farmers' Protests and Repeal
events.description: A fourteen-month encampment by Punjab and Haryana farmers forced the repeal of three agricultural laws, the largest Modi-era policy reversal.

events.label: India Becomes Fifth-Largest Economy
events.description: India overtook the United Kingdom to become the world's fifth-largest economy by nominal GDP, sixteen years after the IT-driven acceleration began.

events.label: India Becomes Most Populous Nation
events.description: India surpassed China to become the world's most populous nation, with a median age of 28 and an open demographic-dividend window.

events.label: Chandrayaan-3 Lands on the Moon
events.description: India's robotic lander touched down near the lunar south pole, the first agency to do so, at a total cost of about $75 million.

events.label: India's G20 Presidency
events.description: India hosted the G20 summit and produced a consensus Delhi Declaration despite Russia-Ukraine tensions, the diplomatic high-water mark of the second term.

events.label: Ram Temple Consecrated in Ayodhya
events.description: Modi personally consecrated the Ram temple, completing the symbolic project the VHP had launched in 1986 and vindicating the BJP's long political bet.

events.label: The 2024 General Election
events.description: The BJP fell to 240 seats and lost its outright majority, requiring NDA coalition management for Modi's third term.

events.label: Modi 3.0 Coalition Government
events.description: The third Modi government operates under coalition constraints not faced since 1999, with major signature legislation deferred.

events.label: Aditya-L1 Solar Mission
events.description: India's first solar observatory reached the Sun-Earth L1 point, extending the low-cost ISRO program beyond the Moon and Mars.

events.label: UPI Digital Payments at Scale
events.description: Unified Payments Interface volume exceeded 10 billion monthly transactions, the world's largest real-time digital-payments system, accelerated by demonetization.

events.label: Record Diaspora Remittances
events.description: India received about $125 billion in remittances, the largest annual remittance receipt of any country in history, from a 32-million-strong diaspora.

events.label: Atmanirbhar Bharat
events.description: The self-reliance initiative reframed industrial and defense policy around indigenous capacity amid the strategic competition with China.
